title: Charred Velvety Thai Rigatoni Moo-Ping
description: A luxurious take on traditional Thai moo-ping, with charred rigatoni and a velvety sauce. Perfect for a special occasion or dinner party.

ingredients.name: Fish sauce
ingredients.notes: make sure it's free from additives

ingredients.name: Rigatoni
ingredients.notes: make sure it's made from high-quality durum wheat

instructions: Meanwhile, cook the rigatoni in a large pot of boiling, salted water until al dente, then reserve.
In a small bowl, whisk together fish sauce, soy sauce, brown sugar, and rice vinegar. Set aside.
Heat 1 tablespoon of olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Sear the pork shoulder until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes per side. Transfer the pork to a plate and set aside.
In the same skillet, add the remaining 1 tablespoon of olive oil. Add the garlic, ginger, and green chilies and sauté for 2 minutes, until fragrant.
Add the Thai red curry paste and sauté for 1 minute, until the paste is fragrant.
Add the coconut cream and whisk until smooth. Bring the mixture to a simmer and cook for 2-3 minutes, until slightly thickened.
Add the reserved rigatoni and toss to coat with the curry sauce.
Add the caramelized pork, fish sauce mixture, and chopped cilantro. Toss everything together until the sauce is evenly distributed.
Transfer the rigatoni mixture to a serving platter and garnish with additional cilantro, green chilies, and kaffir lime leaves, if desired.
